Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/3/1 (set-1)

  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Q. 14 is a case study based problem with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question Nos. 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex}

 2
2.

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}e^{\frac{d y} {d x}}{/tex} = x2.

 2
3.

Write the projection of the vector {tex}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.

 2
4.

If the distance of the point (1, 1, 1) from the plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 is {tex}\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}, find the value(s) of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

 2
5.

Two cards are drawn successively with replacement from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the probability distribution of the number of spade cards.

 2
6.

A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die.

 2

OR

The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex}. What is the probability that the target will be hit, if each one of A and B shoots at the target?

SECTION - B

Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex}

 3
8.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex}, given y(1) = 0.

 3

OR

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).
9.

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.

 3

OR

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}, \vec{\mathrm{b}}=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \text { and } \vec{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.
10.

Show that the lines: {tex}\frac{1-x}{2}=\frac{y-3}{4}=\frac{\mathrm{z}}{-1}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{2 \mathrm{y}-2}{-4}{/tex} = z - 1 are coplanar.

 3

SECTION - C

Question Nos. 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the area of the region bounded by curve 4x2 = y and the line y = 8x + 12, using integration.

 4
12.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex}.

 4

OR

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{-2}^1 \sqrt{5-4 x-x^2} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}
13.

Find the distance of the point (1, -2, 9) from the point of intersection of the line {tex}​​\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+{/tex} {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 10.

 4

Case Study Problem :
14.

A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively.

Based on the above information:

(i)

Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate;

 2
(ii)

Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates.

 2

Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/3/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Q. 14 is a case study based problem with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question Nos. 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex}

Solution:

We have x2 - 6x + 13 = x2 - 6x + 32 - 32 + 13 = (x - 3)2 + 4
So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
Therefore, 
{tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex}

 2
2.

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}e^{\frac{d y} {d x}}{/tex} = x2.

Solution:

{tex}e^{\frac{d y} {d x}}{/tex} = x2
{tex}\frac {dy}{dx}{/tex} = log x2 = 2log x
{tex}\int d y=2 \int \log x d x{/tex}
y= 2[log x {tex}\times{/tex} x - {tex}\int 1 d x{/tex}]
y = 2[x log x - x] + C

 2
3.

Write the projection of the vector {tex}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec a=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\overrightarrow k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-\widehat j+4\overrightarrow k\;{/tex}
{tex}\left(\vec b+\vec c\right)=3\hat i+\hat j+2\vec k\;{/tex}
Now, projection of (b+c) on a = {tex}\frac{\left(\vec b+\vec c\right).\vec a}{\left|\vec a\right|}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\left(3\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k\right)\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{6-2+2}{\sqrt9}=\frac63=2{/tex}

 2
4.

If the distance of the point (1, 1, 1) from the plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 is {tex}\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}, find the value(s) of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

Solution:

P(1,1,1) and equation of plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
Now, distance between a point and plane
{tex}D=\frac{Ax_1+By_1+Cz_1-D}{\sqrt{A^2+B^2+C^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1-1+1+\lambda}{\sqrt{1+1+1}}{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1+\lambda}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}\lambda=4{/tex}​​​​​​​

 2
5.

Two cards are drawn successively with replacement from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the probability distribution of the number of spade cards.

Solution:

Let X derigte the number of spades in a sample of 2 drawn cards from a well shuffle pack of 52 cards.
then X can take the values 0, 1, 2.
P(X = 0) {tex}\neq{/tex} P(no spade)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{39} C_2}{{}^{52}C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{741}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{19}{34}{/tex}
P(X = 1) = P(one spade card)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_1 \times{ }^{39} C_1}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{507}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{13}{34}{/tex}
P(X = 2) = P(both cards ane spade)
{tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_2}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{78}{1326}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{17}{/tex}

Thus, the probability distribution of X is given by 

X 0 1 2
f(X) {tex}\frac{19}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac{13}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac1{17}{/tex}
2
6.

A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die.

Solution:

Let A be the event having sum 7 on the dice
A = (1, 6), (6, 1), (5, 2), (2, 5), (4, 3), (3, 4)
n(A) = 6
Let B be the event in which 5 appears atleast on one die
B = (1, 5), (2, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5), (5, 5), (6, 5), (5, 1), (5, 2), (5, 3), (5, 4), (5, 6)
n(B) = 11
A{tex}\cap{/tex}B = (2, 5), (5, 2)
{tex}P\left(\frac BA\right)=\frac{n\left(A\cap B\right)}{n\left(A\right)}=\frac26=\frac13{/tex}
So, the probability that 5 appears atleast on one dice is {tex}\frac13{/tex}

 2

OR

The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex}. What is the probability that the target will be hit, if each one of A and B shoots at the target?

Solution:

P(A) = P(A hits target) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
P(B) = P(B hits target) = {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex}
Now, Target will be hit if atleast one of them hits the target. Then, required probability is given by,
P(A {tex}\cup{/tex}B) = P (target will be hit by either A or B)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B){/tex} = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)P(B) [A and B are independent]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{5}-\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{5+6-2}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{9}{15}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

 2

SECTION - B

Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} ...(i)
Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin\left(2\pi-x\right)}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{dx}{1+e^{-\sin x}}}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+\frac1{e^{\sin x}}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{\left(1+e^{\sin x}\right)dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{2\pi}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[2\pi-0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\pi{/tex}

 3
8.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex}, given y(1) = 0.

Solution:

{tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
It is form of linear differential equation
So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex}
IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex}
General solution
y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex}
{tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex}
y = xex + Cx ...(i)
Put x = 1, and y = 0
0 = e + C
C = e
Putting in eqn (i)
y = xex + xe​​​​​​

 3

OR

Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).

Solution:

{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1)
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b)
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex}
Integrating both sides
log (logv) = log x + log C
log (logv) = logxC
log v = Cx
{tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx

 3
9.

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.

Solution:

Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}, {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}

{tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex}​​​​​​​
Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex}​​​​​​​
ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex}​​​​​​
ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit

 3

OR

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}, \vec{\mathrm{b}}=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \text { and } \vec{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.

Solution:

Here we have,  {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} 
Now, we have
{tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} 
If {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then we get
{tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}[(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}] \cdot(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 - {tex}\lambda{/tex})3 + (2 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex})1 + (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex})0 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 - 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 8
Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is 8.

 3
10.

Show that the lines: {tex}\frac{1-x}{2}=\frac{y-3}{4}=\frac{\mathrm{z}}{-1}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{2 \mathrm{y}-2}{-4}{/tex} = z - 1 are coplanar.

Solution:

We know that the lines {tex}\frac{x-x_1}{a_1}=\frac{y-y_1}{b_1}=\frac{z-z_1}{c_1}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-x_2}{a_2}=\frac{y-y_2}{b_2}=\frac{z-z_2}{c_2}{/tex}
are copplanar if {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x_2-x_1 & y_2-y_1 & z_2-z_1 \\ a_1 & b_1 & c_1 \\ a_2 & b_2 & c_2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
And the equation of the plane containing these lines is {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x-x_1 & y-y_1 & z-z_1 \\ a_1 & b_1 & c_1 \\ a_2 & b_2 & c_2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
We have, {tex}\frac{1-x}2=\frac{y-3}4=\frac z{-1}\;\&\;\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{2y-2}{-4}=\frac{z-1}1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x-1}{-2}=\frac{y-3}4=\frac{z-0}{-1}\;\&\;\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{y-1}{-2}=\frac{z-1}1{/tex}
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}4-1&1-3&1\\-2&4&-1\\3&-2&1\end{vmatrix}=\begin{vmatrix}3&-2&1\\-2&4&-1\\3&-2&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
= 3(4 - 2) + 2(-2 + 3) + (4 - 12)
= 6 + 2 - 8 = 0
So both lines are coplanar.

 3

SECTION - C

Question Nos. 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the area of the region bounded by curve 4x2 = y and the line y = 8x + 12, using integration.

Solution:

4x2 = y, y = 8x + 12

For intersection point
4x2 = 8x + 12
x2 - 2x - 3 = 0
x = 3, -1
Now, Required area = {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^3y_{line}dx-\int_\limits{-1}^3y_{para}dx{/tex}
Area = {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^3\left(8x+12-4x^2\right)dx=4\int_\limits{-1}^3\left(2x+3-x^2\right){/tex}
{tex}=4\left[x^2+3x-\frac{x^3}3\right]_{-1}^3{/tex}
{tex}=4\left[9+9-9-\left(1-3+\frac13\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=4\left[11-\frac13\right]=4\times\frac{32}3{/tex}
Area = {tex}\frac{128}3{/tex} sq.unit

 4
12.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex}.

Solution:

We will solve this integral by partial fraction
Let{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = (Ax + B) (3x2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x2 + 1)
= (3A + C)x3 + (3B + D) x2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D
Equating similar terms, we get,
3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0
Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4
Thus,
I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
= - tan-1x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex}- tan-1x + c

 4

OR

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_{-2}^1 \sqrt{5-4 x-x^2} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x^2+4x+4-4-5\right)}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x+2\right)^2+9}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex}
As, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac x2\sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}2\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac xa}+C{/tex}
So, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac{x+2}2\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac{x+2}3}+C{/tex}

13.

Find the distance of the point (1, -2, 9) from the point of intersection of the line {tex}​​\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+7 \hat{\mathrm{k}}+{/tex} {tex}\lambda(3 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 10.

Solution:

Given line is {tex}\vec{r}=(4 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+7 \hat{k})+\lambda(3 \hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
Any point on the line is
{tex}(4+3 \lambda, 2+4 \lambda, 7+2 \lambda){/tex}
This point lies on the plane {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\imath}-\hat{\jmath}+\hat{k})=10{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 + 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 2 - 4{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 7 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 9 = 10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Point of intersection is (7, 6, 9)
Distance of (7, 6, 9) from (1, -2, 9) is
d = {tex}\sqrt{(7-1)^2+(6+2)^2+0}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{36+64}{/tex} = 10

Case Study Problem :
14.

A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively.

  1. Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate. (2)
  2. Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates. (2)
Solution:

  1. We have, A1 : A2 : A4 = 4 : 4 : 2
    P(A1) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex}, P(A2) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} and P(A3) = {tex}\frac{2}{10}{/tex}
    where A1, A2 and A3 denote the three types of flower seed.
    Let E be the event that a seed germinates and {tex}\bar {E}{/tex} be the event that a seed does not germinate.
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {45} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {60} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {35} {100}{/tex}
    And {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {55} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {40} {100}{/tex}, {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {65} {100}{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(E) = P(A1) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} + P(A2) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} + P(A3) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{1000}=\frac{490}{1000}{/tex} = 0.49

  2. {tex}P (\frac {A_2} {E}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})}{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_1\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_1}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_3\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_3}})}{/tex}
    {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{60}{100}}{\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{100}}{/tex} 
    = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{490}{1000}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {24} {49}{/tex} = 0.48

Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}{/tex} Solution: We have x 2 - 6x + 13 = x 2 - 6x + 3 2 - 3 2 + 13 = (x - 3) 2 + 4 So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex} 2 2. Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}e^{\frac{d y} {d x}}{/tex} = x 2 . Solution: {tex}e^{\frac{d y} {d x}}{/tex} = x 2 {tex}\frac {dy}{dx}{/tex} = log x 2 = 2log x {tex}\int d y=2 \int \log x d x{/tex} y= 2[log x {tex}\times{/tex} x - {tex}\int 1 d x{/tex} ] y = 2[x log x - x] + C 2 3. Write the projection of the vector {tex}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} . Solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec a=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\overrightarrow k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-\widehat j+4\overrightarrow k\;{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec b+\vec c\right)=3\hat i+\hat j+2\vec k\;{/tex} Now, projection of (b+c) on a = {tex}\frac{\left(\vec b+\vec c\right).\vec a}{\left|\vec a\right|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\left(3\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k\right)\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{6-2+2}{\sqrt9}=\frac63=2{/tex} 2 4. If the distance of the point (1, 1, 1) from the plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 is {tex}\frac{5}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} , find the value(s) of {tex}\lambda{/tex} . Solution: P(1,1,1) and equation of plane x - y + z + {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 Now, distance between a point and plane {tex}D=\frac{Ax_1+By_1+Cz_1-D}{\sqrt{A^2+B^2+C^2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1-1+1+\lambda}{\sqrt{1+1+1}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac5{\sqrt3}=\frac{1+\lambda}{\sqrt3}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\lambda=4{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 2 5. Two cards are drawn successively with replacement from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the probability distribution of the number of spade cards. Solution: Let X derigte the number of spades in a sample of 2 drawn cards from a well shuffle pack of 52 cards. then X can take the values 0, 1, 2. P(X = 0) {tex}\neq{/tex} P(no spade) {tex}=\frac{{ }^{39} C_2}{{}^{52}C_2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{741}{1326}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{19}{34}{/tex} P(X = 1) = P(one spade card) {tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_1 \times{ }^{39} C_1}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{507}{1326}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{13}{34}{/tex} P(X = 2) = P(both cards ane spade) {tex}=\frac{{ }^{13} C_2}{{ }^{52} C_2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{78}{1326}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{17}{/tex} Thus, the probability distribution of X is given by X 0 1 2 f(X) {tex}\frac{19}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac{13}{34}{/tex} {tex}\frac1{17}{/tex} 2 6. A pair of dice is thrown and the sum of the numbers appearing on the dice is observed to be 7. Find the probability that the number 5 has appeared on atleast one die. Solution: Let A be the event having sum 7 on the dice A = (1, 6), (6, 1), (5, 2), (2, 5), (4, 3), (3, 4) n(A) = 6 Let B be the event in which 5 appears atleast on one die B = (1, 5), (2, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5), (5, 5), (6, 5), (5, 1), (5, 2), (5, 3), (5, 4), (5, 6) n(B) = 11 A {tex}\cap{/tex} B = (2, 5), (5, 2) {tex}P\left(\frac BA\right)=\frac{n\left(A\cap B\right)}{n\left(A\right)}=\frac26=\frac13{/tex} So, the probability that 5 appears atleast on one dice is {tex}\frac13{/tex} 2 OR The probability that A hits a target is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} and the probability that B hits it, is {tex}\frac 25{/tex} . What is the probability that the target will be hit, if each one of A and B shoots at the target? Solution: P(A) = P(A hits target) = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} P(B) = P(B hits target) = {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} Now, Target will be hit if atleast one of them hits the target. Then, required probability is given by, P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P (target will be hit by either A or B) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B){/tex} = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)P(B) [A and B are independent] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{5}-\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{5+6-2}{15}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{9}{15}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}P(A \cup B)=\frac{3}{5}{/tex} 2 SECTION - B Question Nos. 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin\left(2\pi-x\right)}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{dx}{1+e^{-\sin x}}}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+\frac1{e^{\sin x}}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{\left(1+e^{\sin x}\right)dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{2\pi}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[2\pi-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\pi{/tex} 3 8. Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex} , given y(1) = 0. Solution: {tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} It is form of linear differential equation So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex} IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex} General solution y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex} {tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex} y = x e x + Cx ...(i) Put x = 1, and y = 0 0 = e + C C = e Putting in eqn (i) y = xe x + xe​​​​​​ 3 OR Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1). Solution: {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1) {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b) Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex} Integrating both sides log (logv) = log x + log C log (logv) = logxC log v = Cx {tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx 3 9. The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} . Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram. Solution: Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex} ​​​​​​​ ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex} ​​​​​​ ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit 3 OR If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}, \vec{\mathrm{b}}=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \text { and } \vec{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} . Solution: Here we have, {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} Now, we have {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} If {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then we get {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}[(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}] \cdot(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 - {tex}\lambda{/tex} )3 + (2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} )1 + (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex} )0 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 - 3 {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 8 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 8 Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is 8. 3 10. Show that the lines: {tex}\frac{1-x}{2}=\frac{y-3}{4}=\frac{\mathrm{z}}{-1}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{2 \mathrm{y}-2}{-4}{/tex} = z - 1 are coplanar. Solution: We know that the lines {tex}\frac{x-x_1}{a_1}=\frac{y-y_1}{b_1}=\frac{z-z_1}{c_1}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-x_2}{a_2}=\frac{y-y_2}{b_2}=\frac{z-z_2}{c_2}{/tex} are copplanar if {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x_2-x_1 & y_2-y_1 & z_2-z_1 \\ a_1 & b_1 & c_1 \\ a_2 & b_2 & c_2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} And the equation of the plane containing these lines is {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} x-x_1 & y-y_1 & z-z_1 \\ a_1 & b_1 & c_1 \\ a_2 & b_2 & c_2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} We have, {tex}\frac{1-x}2=\frac{y-3}4=\frac z{-1}\;\&\;\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{2y-2}{-4}=\frac{z-1}1{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-1}{-2}=\frac{y-3}4=\frac{z-0}{-1}\;\&\;\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{y-1}{-2}=\frac{z-1}1{/tex} {tex}\begin{vmatrix}4-1&1-3&1\\-2&4&-1\\3&-2&1\end{vmatrix}=\begin{vmatrix}3&-2&1\\-2&4&-1\\3&-2&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 3(4 - 2) + 2(-2 + 3) + (4 - 12) = 6 + 2 - 8 = 0 So both lines are coplanar. 3 SECTION - C Question Nos. 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 11. Find the area of the region bounded by curve 4x 2 = y and the line y = 8x + 12, using integration. A shopkeeper sells three types of flower seeds A1, A2, A3. They are sold in the form of a mixture, where the proportions of these seeds are 4 : 4 : 2, respectively. The germination rates of the three types of seeds are 45%, 60% and 35% respectively. Calculate the probability that a randomly chosen seed will germinate. (2) Calculate the probability that the seed is of type A2, given that a randomly chosen seed germinates. (2) Solution: We have, A 1 : A 2 : A 4 = 4 : 4 : 2 P(A1) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} , P(A 2 ) = {tex}\frac{4}{10}{/tex} and P(A 3 ) = {tex}\frac{2}{10}{/tex} where A 1 , A 2 and A 3 denote the three types of flower seed. Let E be the event that a seed germinates and {tex}\bar {E}{/tex} be the event that a seed does not germinate. {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {45} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {60} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {35} {100}{/tex} And {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_1}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {55} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_2}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {40} {100}{/tex} , {tex}P (\frac {\bar E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac {65} {100}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(E) = P(A 1 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_1}){/tex} + P(A 2 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_2}){/tex} + P(A 3 ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}P (\frac {E} {A_3}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{1000}=\frac{490}{1000}{/tex} = 0.49 {tex}P (\frac {A_2} {E}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})}{\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_1\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_1}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_2\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_2}})+\mathrm P\left({\mathrm A}_3\right)\cdot\mathrm P({\displaystyle\frac{\mathrm E}{{\mathrm A}_3}})}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\frac{4}{10} \times \frac{60}{100}}{\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{45}{100}+\frac{4}{10} \cdot \frac{60}{100}+\frac{2}{10} \cdot \frac{35}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{180}{1000}+\frac{240}{1000}+\frac{70}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{240}{1000}}{\frac{490}{1000}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {24} {49}{/tex} = 0.48 4
